---
title: "Corvus Star Phase 1 Implementation Backlog"
date: 2026-03-09
status: completed
---

# Corvus Star Phase 1 Implementation Backlog

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This backlog operationalizes Phase 1 from `docs/architecture/WOVEN_SKILL_CONSOLIDATION_BLUEPRINT.qmd`.
> Phase 1 is the spine freeze.
> It does not attempt full migration.

## Phase 1 Mission

Phase 1 exists to make the target woven-skill architecture executable.

The required outcomes are:

1. A single skill execution contract exists.
2. A single intelligence bridge contract exists.
3. The Hall of Records has a canonical operational schema.
4. The Gungnir matrix has one authoritative field model.
5. Beads are structured records, not markdown-derived authority.
6. TALIESIN emits validation-ready candidates.
7. Validation and SPRT return a canonical promotion verdict.
8. Legacy daemons are demoted to wrappers, not authorities.
9. The active runtime surface typechecks cleanly and Python test collection is green.

## Phase 1 Exit Criteria

- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes for the authoritative runtime surface, or non-authoritative legacy surfaces are explicitly isolated.
- `.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests/unit tests/integration tests/empire_tests tests/crucible -q` exits cleanly.
- `cstar.ts` and command spokes act as dispatch shells only.
- Python and TypeScript share one Mimir bridge request and response shape.
- Hall records, Gungnir scores, beads, and validation results all have stable schemas.
- `tasks.qmd` is projection only.
- PennyOne reads projections derived from Hall records.

## Execution Status

Current Phase 1 state:

- `CS-P1-00` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-01` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-02` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-03` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-04` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-05` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-06` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-07` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-08` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-09` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-10` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-11` closed on 2026-03-09
- `CS-P1-12` closed on 2026-03-09
- Phase 1 completed on 2026-03-09
- Current next ticket: none

## Ticket Index

| ID | Status | Priority | Ticket | Depends On | Primary Surfaces |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `CS-P1-00` | Closed | P0 | Baseline Integrity Recovery | none | TS compile surface, uplink tests |
| `CS-P1-01` | Closed | P0 | Skill Runtime Contract | `CS-P1-00` | CLI shell, command spokes, runtime dispatch |
| `CS-P1-02` | Closed | P0 | Intelligence Bridge Unification | `CS-P1-00` | Mimir bridge, gateway, uplink |
| `CS-P1-03` | Closed | P0 | Hall Schema Canonicalization | `CS-P1-01`, `CS-P1-02` | SQLite state, Hall records |
| `CS-P1-04` | Closed | P1 | Gungnir Matrix Contract | `CS-P1-03` | metrics, universal calculus, PennyOne types |
| `CS-P1-05` | Closed | P1 | Sovereign Bead System | `CS-P1-03`, `CS-P1-04` | bead storage, tasks projection |
| `CS-P1-06` | Closed | P1 | TALIESIN Candidate Contract | `CS-P1-01`, `CS-P1-05` | forge entry, skill forging, test generation |
| `CS-P1-07` | Closed | P1 | Validation and SPRT Contract | `CS-P1-04`, `CS-P1-06` | Crucible, fishtest, benchmark, metrics |
| `CS-P1-08` | Closed | P1 | PennyOne Projection Boundary | `CS-P1-03`, `CS-P1-04` | PennyOne database, compiler, command surface |
| `CS-P1-09` | Closed | P2 | Legacy Raven Demotion | `CS-P1-01`, `CS-P1-07` | daemon loops, ravens command |
| `CS-P1-10` | Closed | P2 | Skill Registry Authority Map | `CS-P1-01` | `.agents/skills`, `src/skills/local` |
| `CS-P1-11` | Closed | P1 | Chant Runtime Remap | `CS-P1-01`, `CS-P1-02`, `CS-P1-03` | `chant`, runtime dispatcher, Hall intent records |
| `CS-P1-12` | Closed | P1 | Evolve Skill Promotion | `CS-P1-05`, `CS-P1-06`, `CS-P1-07`, `CS-P1-10` | `evolve`, bead loop, validation, contract promotion |

## Critical Path

The critical path is:

`CS-P1-00 -> CS-P1-01 -> CS-P1-02 -> CS-P1-03 -> CS-P1-04 -> CS-P1-05 -> CS-P1-06 -> CS-P1-07`

`CS-P1-08` can begin once Hall and Gungnir contracts are stable.

`CS-P1-09` and `CS-P1-10` should start only after the new runtime contract exists.

`CS-P1-11` should begin after the runtime, intelligence bridge, and Hall schema are stable enough to carry canonical intent records.

`CS-P1-12` should begin only after beads, TALIESIN candidate output, validation, and skill registry authority are in place.

## Ticket Details

## `CS-P1-00` Baseline Integrity Recovery

### Objective

Recover a trustworthy baseline before freezing new contracts.
Phase 1 should not define canonical interfaces on top of a broken type surface and a failing Python collection pass.

### Files

- `cstar.ts`
- `src/node/gateway/routes/api/mimir.ts`
- `src/node/core/CorvusProcess.ts`
- `src/cstar/core/uplink.py`
- `tests/crucible/test_uplink.py`
- `src/tools/pennyone/mcp-server.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/vis/components/AuraLayers.tsx`
- `src/tools/pennyone/vis/components/NeuralGraph.tsx`
- `src/tools/pennyone/vis/components/NodeLayer.tsx`
- `src/tools/pennyone/vis/components/SelectionPanelShell.tsx`
- `src/tools/pennyone/vis/proxy.ts`

### Change Set

- Repair or isolate the current TS compile failures.
- Restore alignment between gateway expectations and `CorvusProcess` methods.
- Restore `src/cstar/core/uplink.py` and `tests/crucible/test_uplink.py` API parity.
- Decide whether broken PennyOne UI modules are Phase 1 authority or temporary non-authoritative legacy.

### Acceptance Criteria

- The runtime authority surface is typechecked.
- Python test collection passes without import errors.
- Any excluded legacy surfaces are explicitly documented as transitional.

### Verification

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests/unit tests/integration tests/empire_tests tests/crucible -q
```

## `CS-P1-01` Skill Runtime Contract

### Objective

Make the top-level runtime a dispatch shell over woven skills instead of a business-logic owner.

### Files

- `cstar.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/start.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/dispatcher.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/pennyone.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/ravens.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/vitals.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/mcp.ts`
- `src/node/core/state.ts`
- `src/node/core/CorvusProcess.ts`
- New: `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- New: `src/node/core/runtime/dispatcher.ts`

### Change Set

- Define `SkillInvocation`, `WeaveInvocation`, `WeaveResult`, and `RuntimeContext`.
- Move command-specific business logic into runtime dispatch or skill adapters.
- Leave command files responsible only for CLI parsing, validation, and dispatch.
- Update framework state so it records weave execution rather than daemon status semantics.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No command spoke contains domain logic beyond argument handling.
- A single runtime dispatcher accepts named weaves and structured payloads.
- `cstar start`, `cstar ravens`, and `cstar pennyone` all pass through the same dispatch contract.

### Verification

- Add unit tests for runtime dispatch selection.
- Add command tests that assert arguments are converted into `WeaveInvocation`.

## `CS-P1-02` Intelligence Bridge Unification

### Objective

Define one canonical request and response contract for the Host Agent bridge.
Every Python and TypeScript caller must use the same payload shape.

### Files

- `src/core/mimir_client.py`
- `src/core/mimir_client.ts`
- `src/cstar/core/uplink.py`
- `src/node/gateway/routes/api/mimir.ts`
- `src/node/core/CorvusProcess.ts`
- New: `src/types/intelligence-contract.ts`
- New: `tests/unit/test_intelligence_contract.py`
- New: `tests/node/intelligence-contract.test.ts`

### Change Set

- Standardize request fields for prompt, system prompt, transport mode, correlation id, and caller metadata.
- Standardize response fields for status, raw text, parsed data, error, and trace metadata.
- Remove mismatched legacy names such as `sampleMind`, `getWellIntent`, and ad hoc result envelopes unless they are implemented as explicit compatibility shims.
- Make `src/cstar/core/uplink.py` a thin adapter over the canonical bridge.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Python and TypeScript clients share the same response envelope.
- Gateway routes do not call methods that do not exist on the current process surface.
- The uplink test surface matches the actual exported API.

### Verification

- Contract roundtrip tests in Python and TypeScript.
- Existing Mimir-related tests updated to one payload shape.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/types/intelligence-contract.ts`
- `src/core/intelligence_contract.py`
- `src/core/mimir_client.ts`
- `src/core/mimir_client.py`
- `src/node/core/CorvusProcess.ts`
- `src/node/gateway/routes/api/mimir.ts`
- `src/cstar/core/uplink.py`

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\unit\test_intelligence_contract.py tests\unit\test_mimir_client.py tests\crucible\test_uplink.py tests\crucible\test_convergence.py -q
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests\unit tests\integration tests\empire_tests tests\crucible -q
```

## `CS-P1-03` Hall Schema Canonicalization

### Objective

Define the Hall of Records as the single operational authority for repository scan state.

### Files

- `src/core/engine/memory_db.py`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/database.ts`
- `src/node/core/state.ts`
- `src/tools/vault.py`
- `.stats/pennyone.db`
- `.stats/gravity.db`
- `.agents/sovereign_state.json`
- New: `src/types/hall.ts`
- New: `src/core/engine/hall_schema.py`
- New: `scripts/migrate_hall_schema.py`

### Change Set

- Define canonical entities for repositories, scans, files, matrix scores, beads, validation runs, skill observations, and projections.
- Merge or demote duplicate authorities currently split across `.stats/pennyone.db`, `.stats/gravity.db`, and ad hoc ledgers.
- Keep `.agents/synapse.db` limited to transport exchange rather than repository truth.
- Make `StateRegistry` read summary state from Hall-backed projections rather than inventing overlapping operational fields.

### Acceptance Criteria

- One schema definition exists for Hall-backed operational records.
- Both Python and TypeScript can read and write the same canonical data model.
- The Hall schema can represent repository baseline, historical scans, bead queue, and validation outcomes.

### Verification

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\migrate_hall_schema.py --dry-run
```

- Add Python and TS tests for schema roundtrip and migration safety.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/types/hall.ts`
- `src/core/engine/hall_schema.py`
- `src/core/engine/memory_db.py`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/database.ts`
- `src/node/core/state.ts`
- `scripts/migrate_hall_schema.py`
- `tests/unit/test_hall_schema.py`
- `tests/unit/test_hall_schema.test.ts`

Workspace migration applied:

- `.stats/pennyone.db` now contains canonical Hall tables and projection view
- migrated `1` repository record
- migrated `582` scan records
- migrated `16` bead records
- migrated `1001` validation records

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\unit\test_hall_schema.py -q
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\migrate_hall_schema.py --dry-run
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests\unit tests\integration tests\empire_tests tests\crucible -q
```

## `CS-P1-04` Gungnir Matrix Contract

### Objective

Unify Gungnir into one field model across metrics, calculus, Hall storage, and PennyOne projection.

### Files

- `src/core/engine/gungnir/universal.py`
- `src/core/metrics.py`
- `src/tools/pennyone/types.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/compiler.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/analyzer.ts`
- New: `src/types/gungnir.ts`
- New: `src/core/engine/gungnir/schema.py`

### Change Set

- Reconcile the current mixed matrix fields with the target axes:
  `logic`, `style`, `intel`, `gravity`, `vigil`, `evolution`, `anomaly`, `sovereignty`.
- Define any derived or legacy fields such as `overall`, `stability`, `coupling`, or `aesthetic` as projections, not canonical axes.
- Ensure Hall records and PennyOne graph compiler consume the same field names.

### Acceptance Criteria

- One canonical Gungnir score object exists across Python and TypeScript.
- PennyOne no longer depends on private field names that diverge from the authoritative schema.
- Repository baseline and per-file matrix records serialize identically in both runtimes.

### Verification

- Add serializer tests for Python and TS.
- Add a matrix compile test that proves PennyOne can render from canonical Hall output.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/types/gungnir.ts`
- `src/core/engine/gungnir/schema.py`
- `src/core/engine/gungnir/universal.py`
- `src/core/metrics.py`
- `src/tools/pennyone/types.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/analyzer.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/index.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/compiler.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/database.ts`
- `src/types/hall.ts`
- `src/core/engine/hall_schema.py`
- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/dispatcher.ts`
- `src/node/core/skills/types.ts`
- `src/node/core/skills/SkillManager.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/run-skill.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_gungnir_schema.py`
- `tests/unit/test_gungnir_schema.test.ts`

Outcome:

- One canonical Gungnir object now exists across Python and TypeScript with authoritative axes:
  `logic`, `style`, `intel`, `gravity`, `vigil`, `evolution`, `anomaly`, `sovereignty`
- Derived fields remain present as projections:
  `overall`, `stability`, `coupling`, `aesthetic`
- PennyOne scan output now writes canonical per-file Gungnir matrices into Hall scan records
- PennyOne graph compilation can now render from canonical Hall file records

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\unit\test_gungnir_schema.py tests\unit\test_metrics_engine.py tests\empire_tests\test_universal_gungnir.py -q
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests\unit tests\integration tests\empire_tests tests\crucible -q
```

## `CS-P1-05` Sovereign Bead System

### Objective

Turn beads into structured units of work and demote `tasks.qmd` to projection status.

### Files

- `src/core/norn_coordinator.py`
- `tasks.qmd`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/database.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/start.ts`
- New: `src/types/bead.ts`
- New: `src/core/engine/bead_ledger.py`
- New: `scripts/project_beads_to_qmd.py`

### Change Set

- Define canonical bead fields:
  `id`, `repo_id`, `scan_id`, `target_path`, `rationale`, `contract_refs`, `baseline_scores`, `acceptance_criteria`, `status`, `assigned_agent`, `created_at`.
- Stop parsing `tasks.qmd` as authoritative input.
- Make `tasks.qmd` a rendered projection of the bead ledger.
- Update target selection to pull from structured bead records rather than markdown order.

### Acceptance Criteria

- `NornCoordinator` reads and writes structured bead records.
- `tasks.qmd` can be regenerated from the ledger.
- A bead always links back to a repository scan and baseline score record.

### Verification

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\project_beads_to_qmd.py --check
```

- Add coordinator tests for open, claim, resolve, and projection sync.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/core/engine/bead_ledger.py`
- `src/core/norn_coordinator.py`
- `src/core/engine/wardens/norn.py`
- `src/skills/local/dormancy.py`
- `src/types/bead.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/database.ts`
- `scripts/project_beads_to_qmd.py`
- `tests/unit/test_bead_ledger.py`
- `tests/unit/test_norn_coordinator_empire.py`
- `tests/empire_tests/test_norn_empire.py`
- `tests/empire_tests/test_norn_campaign_empire.py`
- `tests/contracts/test_muninn.py`
- `tests/unit/test_dormancy_rem.py`

Outcome:

- `hall_beads` is now the canonical bead authority
- `NornCoordinator` and `NornWarden` operate on structured Hall-backed beads instead of parsing markdown
- `tasks.qmd` is now a generated projection of the Sovereign Bead System
- new and migrated beads are normalized to include a scan reference and baseline score context
- dormancy now emits structured evolution beads into the canonical ledger

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\unit\test_bead_ledger.py tests\unit\test_norn_coordinator_empire.py tests\empire_tests\test_norn_empire.py tests\empire_tests\test_norn_campaign_empire.py tests\contracts\test_muninn.py tests\unit\test_dormancy_rem.py -q
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\project_beads_to_qmd.py
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\project_beads_to_qmd.py --check
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests\unit tests\integration tests\empire_tests tests\crucible -q
```

## `CS-P1-06` TALIESIN Candidate Contract

### Objective

Require TALIESIN to operate on bounded bead-driven work with explicit contract and baseline inputs.

### Files

- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin_forge.py`
- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin.py`
- `src/core/engine/skill_forger.py`
- `src/tools/generate_tests.py`
- `.agents/workflows/run-task.md`
- New: `src/types/forge-candidate.ts`
- New: `src/core/engine/forge_candidate.py`

### Change Set

- Define a canonical forge request containing bead id, target path, contract references, baseline scores, and operator constraints.
- Define a canonical forge result containing candidate patch data, generated tests, required validations, and trace metadata.
- Prevent the authoritative forge path from operating directly on freeform lore without a bead and contract boundary.
- Keep experimental lore-driven generation only as a clearly non-authoritative path if needed.

### Acceptance Criteria

- TALIESIN consumes structured bead input.
- TALIESIN output is validation-ready rather than raw staged text only.
- Generated tests are associated with the candidate result envelope.

### Verification

- Add forge request and response envelope tests.
- Add a fixture-driven test proving freeform lore gets normalized into a bead-backed request before execution.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/core/engine/forge_candidate.py`
- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin_forge.py`
- `docs/legacy_archive/src_sentinel/taliesin.py`
- `src/core/engine/skill_forger.py`
- `src/tools/generate_tests.py`
- `.agents/workflows/run-task.md`
- `src/types/forge-candidate.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.py`
- `tests/unit/test_forge_candidate.test.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_taliesin.py`

Outcome:

- TALIESIN now consumes a canonical forge request built from a structured bead
- lore-driven forging is explicitly a transitional adapter that normalizes freeform lore into a bead-backed request before execution
- skill forging now stages validation-ready candidates instead of raw freeform artifacts
- generated tests are attached to the forge result envelope
- required validations and trace metadata now travel with the staged candidate

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\unit\test_forge_candidate.py tests\unit\test_taliesin.py tests\empire_tests\test_generate_tests_empire.py -q
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests\unit tests\integration tests\empire_tests tests\crucible -q
```

## `CS-P1-07` Validation and SPRT Contract

### Objective

Make promotion decisions depend on one validation envelope shared by Crucible, benchmark, Gungnir delta, and SPRT verdicts.

### Files

- `src/core/engine/ravens/muninn_crucible.py`
- `sterileAgent/fishtest.py`
- `src/tools/benchmark_engine.py`
- `src/core/metrics.py`
- `.agents/workflows/test.md`
- New: `src/types/validation.ts`
- New: `src/core/engine/validation_result.py`

### Change Set

- Define `ValidationResult`, `ScoreDelta`, `BenchmarkResult`, and `SprtVerdict`.
- Require the validation stage to emit pre-forge and post-forge Gungnir deltas.
- Require SPRT output to be attached to the candidate promotion record.
- Block promotion on reduced `logic`, `style`, or `sovereignty` unless an explicit override pathway is designed later.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Validation no longer returns ad hoc booleans only.
- Fishtest and Crucible can both contribute to one promotion verdict.
- Promotion logic can explain exactly why a candidate passed or failed.

### Verification

- Add tests for accepted, rejected, and inconclusive verdict envelopes.
- Add a regression test proving a negative Gungnir delta blocks promotion.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/core/engine/validation_result.py`
- `src/types/validation.ts`
- `src/tools/benchmark_engine.py`
- `sterileAgent/fishtest.py`
- `src/core/engine/ravens/muninn_crucible.py`
- `.agents/workflows/test.md`
- `tests/unit/test_validation_result.py`
- `tests/unit/test_validation_result.test.ts`
- `tests/empire_tests/test_fishtest_empire.py`
- `tests/contracts/test_muninn.py`

Outcome:

- promotion decisions now use one canonical validation envelope with protected-axis gating
- Crucible and fishtest both contribute to the same promotion record
- SPRT output is attached to validation records and regression verdicts are explainable
- promotion is blocked on reduced `logic`, `style`, or `sovereignty`

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\unit\test_validation_result.py tests\empire_tests\test_fishtest_empire.py tests\contracts\test_muninn.py tests\crucible\test_muninn_crucible.py -q
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests\unit tests\integration tests\empire_tests tests\crucible -q
```

## `CS-P1-08` PennyOne Projection Boundary

### Objective

Make PennyOne a projection surface over Hall records rather than a parallel authority.

### Files

- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/database.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/compiler.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/types.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/index.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/mcp-server.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/pennyone.ts`
- `.stats/matrix-graph.json`

### Change Set

- Read canonical Hall data first, then build graph payloads from it.
- Treat `matrix-graph.json` as a derived artifact only.
- Remove assumptions that PennyOne owns the score schema.
- Ensure the view stack can survive schema evolution through explicit mapping layers.

### Acceptance Criteria

- PennyOne can rebuild its graph from Hall-backed projection data.
- No PennyOne path is treated as the authoritative source of repository truth.
- Graph compile failures do not corrupt canonical state.

### Verification

- Add projection compile tests from seeded Hall fixtures.
- Add a smoke test for the PennyOne command surface against projected data.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/compiler.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/intel/database.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/index.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/live/search.ts`
- `src/tools/pennyone/mcp-server.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/adapters.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_pennyone_projection_boundary.test.ts`

Outcome:

- PennyOne now projects from Hall-backed records rather than treating `matrix-graph.json` as authority
- graph rebuilds are deterministic and can be regenerated after scans
- live search and file intent reads now resolve through Hall-backed data paths

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests\unit tests\integration tests\empire_tests tests\crucible -q
```

## `CS-P1-09` Legacy Raven Demotion

### Objective

Reduce daemon ownership and convert legacy loops into wrappers around the new runtime contract.

### Files

- `src/sentinel/main_loop.py`
- `src/core/engine/ravens/muninn.py`
- `src/core/engine/ravens/muninn_heart.py`
- `src/core/engine/ravens/muninn_hunter.py`
- `src/node/core/commands/ravens.ts`
- `src/cstar/core/daemon.py`
- `src/cstar/core/rpc.py`

### Change Set

- Extract any remaining exclusive business logic out of daemon loops.
- Make `ravens` commands invoke named weaves or lifecycle wrappers over the skill runtime.
- Mark legacy loop files as transitional wrappers with removal criteria.

### Acceptance Criteria

- No core capability exists only because a daemon loop owns it.
- Raven lifecycle commands become runtime lifecycle commands.
- Legacy bridges are documented as adapters and not treated as target architecture.

### Verification

- Add adapter tests for lifecycle start, stop, and status.
- Document the deprecation boundary inside each wrapper module.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/node/core/runtime/adapters.ts`
- `src/sentinel/main_loop.py`
- `src/core/engine/ravens/muninn.py`
- `src/cstar/core/daemon.py`
- `src/cstar/core/rpc.py`
- `tests/unit/test_ravens_lifecycle.test.ts`

Outcome:

- raven lifecycle actions now execute as runtime adapters instead of command-owned daemon logic
- legacy Python loop files are explicitly marked as transitional wrappers
- the removal boundary for daemon ownership is now visible in code and covered by adapter tests

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\contracts\test_main_loop.py tests\empire_tests\test_main_loop_empire.py tests\crucible\test_muninn.py -q
```

## `CS-P1-10` Skill Registry Authority Map

### Objective

Choose one authoritative skill registry and make the transition measurable.

### Files

- `.agents/skills/`
- `src/skills/local/`
- `src/core/engine/skill_forger.py`
- `src/core/engine/memory_db.py`
- New: `scripts/audit_skill_registry.py`
- New: `docs/reports/SKILL_AUTHORITY_REPORT.qmd`

### Change Set

- Declare `.agents/skills/` the authoritative registry for the woven runtime.
- Classify each `src/skills/local/` skill as migrate, wrap, keep as bootstrap-only, or retire.
- Record provenance for each active skill: authority path, contract path, runtime trigger, and migration status.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Every active skill resolves to one authority path.
- Duplicate skill definitions are identified and classified.
- Skill discovery does not require guessing between `.agents/skills/` and `src/skills/local/`.

### Verification

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\audit_skill_registry.py
```

- Emit a report showing authoritative skills, duplicates, and migration candidates.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `scripts/audit_skill_registry.py`
- `.agents/skill_registry.json`
- `docs/reports/SKILL_AUTHORITY_REPORT.qmd`
- `src/core/engine/skill_forger.py`
- `src/core/engine/memory_db.py`
- `src/node/core/runtime/adapters.ts`
- `src/core/cstar_dispatcher.py`
- `tests/unit/test_skill_registry_audit.py`

Outcome:

- `.agents/skills/` is now the authoritative registry surface for active skills
- dynamic discovery consults the generated registry manifest before legacy local paths
- the current audit records `57` authoritative skills, `8` transitional local entries, and `5` duplicate definitions
- `evolve` now appears as an authoritative skill package in the registry output

Verification completed:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\audit_skill_registry.py
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\unit\test_skill_registry_audit.py -q
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
```

## `CS-P1-11` Chant Runtime Remap

### Objective

Remap `chant` from a legacy Python cognitive router into a first-class runtime-dispatched weave skill.

### Files

- `.agents/skills/chant/SKILL.md`
- `.agents/skills/chant/chant.feature`
- `.agents/skills/chant/scripts/chant.py`
- `cstar.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/dispatcher.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/dispatcher.ts`
- `src/core/cstar_dispatcher.py`
- New: `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/chant.ts`

### Change Set

- Define the canonical `chant` invocation and result envelope.
- Make `cstar chant "<query>"` dispatch through the runtime kernel instead of using the legacy Python dispatcher as the primary authority.
- Keep `chant.py` only as a transitional adapter if Python-side compatibility is still needed.
- Record normalized intent, selected execution path, emitted beads, and child execution summary in Hall-backed records.
- Convert missing-capability detection into a structured runtime result rather than ad hoc subprocess branching.

### Acceptance Criteria

- `cstar chant "<query>"` passes through the runtime dispatcher.
- `chant` no longer depends on `src/core/cstar_dispatcher.py` as the authority surface.
- Hall records capture the normalized chant, selected weave or skill path, and mission outcome.
- `chant.py` is either an adapter over the runtime contract or explicitly marked as transitional.

### Verification

- Add command tests that assert `chant` arguments become a canonical runtime invocation.
- Add runtime tests for structured success, unresolved intent, and missing capability envelopes.
- Add a dry-run or fixture-backed smoke check for `cstar chant`.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/chant.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/bootstrap.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/dispatcher.ts`
- `.agents/skills/chant/scripts/chant.py`
- `.agents/skills/chant/SKILL.md`
- `tests/unit/test_chant_runtime.test.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_runtime_command_invocations.test.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_runtime_dispatch.test.ts`

Outcome:

- `cstar chant "<query>"` now dispatches through the runtime kernel as `weave:chant`
- `chant.py` remains only as a compatibility adapter over the runtime surface
- Hall-backed skill observations capture normalized intent resolution and mission outcome
- unresolved intent and missing-capability paths now return structured runtime envelopes

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
npx tsx cstar.ts chant "ravens status"
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe .agents\skills\chant\scripts\chant.py "ravens status"
```

## `CS-P1-12` Evolve Skill Promotion

### Objective

Promote `evolve` from workflow-only surface to a first-class skill that executes the bead-driven optimization loop under runtime, Hall, and validation authority.

### Files

- `.agents/workflows/evolve.md`
- `.agents/forge_staged/evolve.md`
- `cstar.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/dispatcher.ts`
- New: `.agents/skills/evolve/SKILL.md`
- New: `.agents/skills/evolve/evolve.feature`
- New: `.agents/skills/evolve/contract.json`
- New: `.agents/skills/evolve/scripts/evolve.py`
- New: `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/evolve.ts`

### Change Set

- Forge `.agents/skills/evolve/` as the authoritative skill package.
- Define the canonical `evolve` invocation over bead id, target path, contracts, focus axes, and validation profile.
- Convert `evolve.md` into a projection or operator guide rather than the canonical executor.
- Route `evolve` through the runtime as a bead claim -> forge -> validate -> contract proposal loop.
- Ensure `evolve` writes bead claim and resolution, metric deltas, validation results, and contract proposal records into Hall-backed structures.

### Acceptance Criteria

- `.agents/skills/evolve/` exists and is the authority path for `evolve`.
- `evolve` is invocable through the runtime dispatcher.
- `evolve.md` is no longer the sole execution surface.
- `evolve` records bead claim, validation verdict, SPRT result, and contract proposal or promotion outcome.
- Contract mutation occurs through proposal and promotion boundaries, not silent rewrite.

### Verification

- Add runtime tests for `evolve` invocation and result envelopes.
- Add fixture-backed tests for bead claim -> validation -> proposal flow.
- Add a smoke invocation for `cstar run-skill evolve --params ...` or a dedicated `cstar evolve` command if introduced.

### Closure Note

Closed on 2026-03-09.

Implemented surfaces:

- `src/core/engine/evolve_skill.py`
- `.agents/skills/evolve/SKILL.md`
- `.agents/skills/evolve/evolve.feature`
- `.agents/skills/evolve/contract.json`
- `.agents/skills/evolve/scripts/evolve.py`
- `.agents/workflows/evolve.md`
- `src/node/core/runtime/contracts.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/weaves/evolve.ts`
- `src/node/core/runtime/bootstrap.ts`
- `src/node/core/commands/dispatcher.ts`
- `tests/unit/test_evolve_skill.py`
- `tests/unit/test_evolve_runtime.test.ts`

Outcome:

- `evolve` is now a first-class authoritative skill package under `.agents/skills/evolve/`
- `cstar evolve` dispatches through `weave:evolve` and records Hall-backed observations, validation results, and proposal metadata
- the legacy workflow doc is now an operator guide rather than the canonical executor
- no-action bead conditions return a structured outcome instead of failing the runtime

Verification completed:

```powershell
npx tsc --noEmit
npm run test:node
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests\unit\test_evolve_skill.py tests\unit\test_skill_registry_audit.py -q
npx tsx cstar.ts evolve --dry-run
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe .agents\skills\evolve\scripts\evolve.py --dry-run
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe scripts\audit_skill_registry.py
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest --collect-only tests\unit tests\integration tests\empire_tests tests\crucible -q
```

## Phase 1 Completion Note

Phase 1 closed on 2026-03-09.

Exit criteria satisfied:

- the authoritative TypeScript runtime surface passes `npx tsc --noEmit`
- Python collection now exits cleanly with `268` tests collected
- `cstar.ts` and command spokes are dispatch shells over the runtime kernel
- Hall, Gungnir, beads, validation, chant, and evolve now have explicit authoritative contracts
- PennyOne and `tasks.qmd` operate as projections over canonical records rather than hidden authorities

## Suggested Execution Order

### Sprint 1

- `CS-P1-00`
- `CS-P1-01`
- `CS-P1-02`

### Sprint 2

- `CS-P1-03`
- `CS-P1-04`
- `CS-P1-05`

### Sprint 3

- `CS-P1-06`
- `CS-P1-07`
- `CS-P1-08`

### Sprint 4

- `CS-P1-09`
- `CS-P1-10`
- `CS-P1-11`

### Sprint 5

- `CS-P1-12`

## First Week Cut List

If only the highest-leverage work is done first, the first week should focus on:

1. Recover compile and collection integrity.
2. Define the runtime dispatch contract.
3. Unify the intelligence bridge envelope.
4. Define the Hall schema.
5. Define the canonical Gungnir matrix object.

Without those five moves, the rest of the consolidation will keep drifting.

## Do Not Do During Phase 1

- Do not add new daemon-only pathways.
- Do not add new ad hoc ledgers for state that belongs in Hall records.
- Do not expand PennyOne features before its input schema is stabilized.
- Do not let skill-learning mutation rewrite canonical contracts automatically.
- Do not keep `tasks.qmd` as a hidden task authority.

## Definition of Ready for Phase 2

Phase 2 can begin only when:

- the runtime contract is real
- Hall records are authoritative
- Gungnir is canonical
- beads are structured
- TALIESIN and validation speak the same envelopes
- legacy loops are clearly adapter territory

At that point, the actual migration from hybrid execution to woven execution becomes tractable.
